ink-subtle.note: Doubles as the ICE powertrain chart color. The semantic overlap is intentional: ICE is both neutral text and the neutral baseline powertrain.

accent-bev.note: BEV signal color. Used exclusively for Battery Electric Vehicle data.

headline.purpose: Page title in sticky header. The only 14px text in the system. Compact by design — this is not a marketing surface.

body.purpose: Figcaptions, stat descriptor labels, chart legend text, secondary information.

label.purpose: Chart axis tick labels. Smallest legible text in the system.

mono.purpose: All numeric metric values: stat strip, future data tables, any column of numbers. Always paired with tabular-nums feature.

motion.name: colors-fast
motion.value: transition-property: color, background-color, border-color; transition-duration: 200ms; transition-timing-function: ease-out;
motion.purpose: Default transition — scoped to color properties only, never transition-all.

breakpoints.name: sm
breakpoints.note: Stat strip switches from column to row layout

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.note: Chart heights increase from h-64 to h-80

components.name: Stat Strip
components.description: Compact metric summary in the sticky header. Replaces hero-metric KPI card grids. Three data points: YTD Total (neutral), BEV Share (Electric Teal — the EV story), Top Brand (neutral).
components.html:
YTD Total
622,438
BEV Share
18.4%
Top Brand
Toyota
components.css: .ds-stat-strip { background: #020617; padding: 16px 24px; border-bottom: 1px solid #1e293b; } .ds-stats { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 20px; list-style: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; } .ds-stat { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; } .ds-stat__label { color: #64748b; font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.4; } .ds-stat__value { color: #f1f5f9; font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; letter-spacing: -0.01em; line-height: 1.2; } .ds-stat__value--bev { color: #2dd4bf; } .ds-stat__divider { width: 1px; height: 28px; background: #1e293b; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Chart Panel
components.description: Primary data surface. A structural container — not a decorative card. No shadow, no blur. Border functions as a grid line. The chart data reads without competition from its container.
components.css: .ds-chart-panel { background: #0f172a; border: 1px solid #1e293b; border-radius: 6px; padding: 24px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; height: 320px; margin: 0; } .ds-chart-panel__caption { color: #94a3b8; font-family: 'Geist', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; margin: 0 0 16px 0; } .ds-chart-panel__body { flex: 1; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; }

narrative.overview:
This system reads and behaves like a terminal-native data display. Chrome disappears. Every pixel earns its place by serving the data. Hierarchy does not come from the visual weight of containers — it comes from the numbers themselves, and from the semantic colors assigned to the powertrain categories they represent.

The palette is deep-dark with near-invisible surfaces. Electric Teal signals the most important trend (BEV growth). Hybrid Amber and Plugin Orange tag the supporting categories. ICE — the established majority — is rendered in the same muted slate as secondary text: neutral, present, not highlighted. The system directs the eye toward what is changing, not toward what has always been there.

This system explicitly rejects the aesthetic it replaced: glassmorphism-as-default, gradient text on headings, hero-metric KPI cards with colored icon badges, and the generic SaaS dark-mode palette of blue/purple/emerald that signals AI-generated dashboard from across a room.
keyCharacteristics: Near-invisible surfaces — panels defined by 1px borders, not shadows or blur
Monospaced numeric values (Geist Mono, tabular-nums) for scannable columns
Minimal type scale — 14px/12px/11px; no display or hero sizes
Chart colors carry semantic weight: teal = BEV, amber = HEV, orange = PHEV, slate = ICE
Key metrics live in the sticky header stat strip, not in a grid of KPI cards

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Electric Teal (#2dd4bf) represents BEV data, and nothing else. It is not a general-purpose accent. Any new feature that needs a highlight color should use weight, position, or a border — not teal.

rules.name: The ICE-as-Neutral Rule
rules.body: ICE vehicles are the established baseline; they are visually subordinate. The eye lands on teal first, then amber, then orange. ICE arrives last because it requires no signal. This color assignment is non-negotiable.

rules.name: The No-General-Accent Rule
rules.body: This system has no general-purpose UI accent color. State indicators use increased weight or a border treatment. The three chart colors are data colors, not UI colors.

rules.name: The No-Display Rule
rules.body: There are no display or hero heading sizes in this system. The largest text is the page title at 14px. Adding a clamp-scaled h1 or a 3rem hero is prohibited.

rules.name: The Mono-for-Numbers Rule
rules.body: Every numeric metric uses Geist Mono with tabular-nums. Proportional figures are never used for data.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: If you are about to add box-shadow, stop. Use border: 1px solid #1e293b instead. Elevation is tonal: a lighter surface within a darker container. No exceptions in the data display layer.

dos: Use <figure aria-label="..."> + <figcaption> on every chart panel
Use Geist Mono with tabular-nums and font-weight: 600 for every numeric metric value
Keep chart color assignments semantically constant: Electric Teal = BEV, Hybrid Amber = HEV, Plugin Orange = PHEV, Ink Subtle = ICE
Use border: 1px solid #1e293b as the only surface separator
Derive all displayed metric values from actual data; show — when data is missing
Scope transitions to the exact properties that change: transition-colors duration-200
Respect prefers-reduced-motion — the rule is in globals.css
donts: Don't use backdrop-filter: blur() or backdrop-blur on data surfaces — glassmorphism is prohibited
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text with a gradient background) — banned unconditionally
Don't add a hero-metric KPI card grid — summary metrics live in the stat strip
Don't use #3b82f6 blue, #8b5cf6 purple, or #10b981 emerald as general UI accents
Don't add decorative background blobs, radial gradients, or animated glow effects
Don't hardcode metric values — any static number that is not a design constant is a data integrity bug
Don't use transition-all — scope to transition-colors
Don't build loading states as a centered spinner — use a skeleton layout or inline text placeholder
Don't add pulsing 'Live Data Sync' badges unless the data stream is genuinely real-time
Don't use display fonts, hero sizing, or clamp-scaled headings
